target: WIN-067 detail_evidence — fix incorrect DOI and citation for Ebbing/Pappa Antarctic gravity paper
rationale: DOI 10.1029/2019JB018855 resolves to Castellanos et al. (2020) "Using a Time-Based Subarray Method to Extract and Invert Noise-Derived Body Waves at Long Beach, California" — completely unrelated to Antarctic gravity. The intended citation is Pappa, Ebbing, Ferraccioli & van der Wal (2019) "Modeling Satellite Gravity Gradient Data to Derive Density, Temperature, and Viscosity Structure of the Antarctic Lithosphere" (DOI: 10.1029/2019JB017997). Ebbing is second author not first; publication year is 2019 not 2021. The AGU DOI encodes submission year (2019) which matches the publication year in this case.
holes_addressed: ISS-683: Incorrect DOI for Ebbing citation in WIN-067
new_evidence_added: Confirmed correct DOI via web search: 10.1029/2019JB017997 (Pappa et al. 2019 JGR Solid Earth)

anticipated_objections.objection: Maybe there is a separate Ebbing et al. 2021 paper we should cite instead
anticipated_objections.response: Web search found no Ebbing-first-author 2021 JGR paper on Antarctic gravity. The 2019 Pappa et al. paper with Ebbing as co-author is the canonical reference for satellite gravity gradient modeling of Antarctic lithospheric structure. If a better 2021 reference exists, curmudgeon can flag it in review.

kernel_of_truth: The scientific claim supported by this citation — that Antarctic gravity anomalies have well-characterized standard-physics causes including crustal thickness variations and sub-lithospheric mantle structure — is correct. Only the citation metadata (DOI, author order, year) needs correction.
